Politics.headline: Trump Imposes Naval Blockade on Iran After Peace Talks Collapse
Politics.detail: Direct negotiations between the U.S. and Iran, mediated by Pakistan, have ended without a deal, leading President Trump to impose a naval blockade.

Politics.headline: US Appeals Court Extends Deadline to Halt White House Ballroom Construction
Politics.detail: A US appeals court has extended the deadline regarding the halting of construction on the White House ballroom.

Politics.headline: Vance Leaves Pakistan After Iran Peace Talks Break Down
Politics.detail: Vice President JD Vance announced that the U.S. and Iran failed to reach a deal after peace talks in Islamabad, citing disagreements over nuclear weapons development.

Politics.headline: Manhattan DA Investigating Eric Swalwell Over Sexual Assault Allegations
Politics.detail: The Manhattan District Attorney's office is reportedly investigating Eric Swalwell over allegations of sexual assault, adding another layer to the controversy surrounding the California representative.

Politics.headline: Trump Reportedly Says He'll Issue Mass Pardons at End of His Presidential Term
Politics.detail: Donald Trump has reportedly said he will issue pardons en masse to his closest advisors at the end of his second presidency, promising them in casual conversations over the last year.

Finance & Economy.headline: Economic shock of Middle East war to cast shadow over IMF, World Bank meetings
Finance & Economy.detail: Global finance leaders are meeting amid the Middle East war, a third major shock impacting the world economy, leading the IMF and World Bank to downgrade growth forecasts and raise inflation predictions, especially for emerging markets. Vulnerable nations require significant emergency support as debt levels soar.

Finance & Economy.headline: Lessons learned in '70s have made US, world economies less vulnerable to oil shocks
Finance & Economy.detail: The world economy is experiencing a flashback to the 1970s with surging oil prices due to the Middle East war, threatening stagflation, but the U.S. and world economies are less vulnerable than during the 1973 oil crisis. Cars, planes, trucks and ships still get about 90% of their delivered energy from petroleum.

Finance & Economy.headline: World finance chiefs head to IMF with a sense of deja vu
Finance & Economy.detail: Economic policymakers are gathering in Washington to assess the damage U.S. President Donald Trump's war on Iran has caused to growth in the Middle East and beyond. The IMF and World Bank's spring meetings will focus on U.S.-Iranian negotiations and how governments and central banks can best support their economies without creating new problems.

Finance & Economy.headline: China, Iran weaponized the global economy to beat the U.S. at its own game
Finance & Economy.detail: China and Iran have weaponized the global economy, exploiting dependencies to pressure the U.S.. China used its dominance in rare earth minerals, and Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, impacting global energy markets.

Finance & Economy.headline: Task for the week: limit the fallout from biggest oil shock in decades
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices and borrowing costs are expected to rise as tankers remain stranded in the Persian Gulf. The article discusses limiting the economic fallout from the biggest oil shock in decades.

Technology.headline: Artemis II returns to Earth after successful Pacific splashdown
Technology.detail: The four Artemis II astronauts safely returned to Earth, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean off San Diego after re-entry into the atmosphere, marking a milestone toward the lunar program with Artemis III planned soon. The crew was transferred to the USS John P. Murtha for medical checks and are reported to be in good condition.

Technology.headline: Tech-powered health: High-end medical frontiers at CICPE 2026
Technology.detail: The 6th China International Consumer Products Expo (CICPE) showcases a wide array of cutting-edge medical technologies and high-end healthcare products, including robotic rehabilitation systems and pioneering diagnostic kits. The International Health Consumption Zone highlights the integration of innovations into clinical practice to meet real-world patient needs.

Technology.headline: Google Rolls Out E2EE Encryption for iOS and Android
Technology.detail: Gmail users can now send encrypted messages to any recipient, even if they use an email service other than Gmail. However, it's only available to Workspace users with an Enterprise Plus subscription and the Assured Controls or Assured Controls Plus add-on, where admins have enabled the tool.

Technology.headline: Police arrest a suspect in a Molotov attack at OpenAI CEO's San Francisco home
Technology.detail: A suspect has been arrested in connection with a Molotov cocktail attack at the San Francisco home of OpenAI's CEO. The investigation is ongoing, and details are limited at this time.

Technology.headline: The AI divide putting open weights models in spotlight
Technology.detail: Smaller open AI models from Google, Alibaba, Microsoft, and Nvidia are proving competitive and relatively cheap to run, creating opportunities for companies of all sizes to implement AI. These models can retrieve information from the web, databases, and APIs, and take action based on the results through tool calls.

Science.headline: The Universe is expanding too fast and scientists still can't explain it
Science.detail: A new measurement confirms the universe's expansion rate is faster than early-Universe models predict, deepening the "Hubble tension" and suggesting our current model of the cosmos may be incomplete.

Science.headline: Neandertals may have hunted and eaten outsiders, chilling cannibalism study finds
Science.detail: A cave in Belgium revealed evidence suggesting Neandertals selectively cannibalized outsiders, particularly women and children, butchering their bones for meat and marrow, indicating practical reasons or intergroup conflict rather than ritual.

Science.headline: South Africa eyes deeper sci-tech cooperation with China, says official
Science.detail: South Africa aims to strengthen its collaboration with China in AI, clean energy, and youth exchanges, supported by the countries' strategic partnership. Minister Blade Nzimande highlighted AI and clean energy as key areas for future cooperation, emphasizing the importance of joint research programs.

Science.headline: China welcomes global cooperation in space
Science.detail: China's Tiangong space station is fully operational and open to global collaboration, with numerous scientific projects underway involving multiple countries. The Chang'e-7 mission will carry payloads from several countries, and China is actively sharing space technology and opportunities with other nations.

Science.headline: Every tire produces a chemical that kills coho salmon. Can Northwest scientists pump the brakes?
Science.detail: Scientists are investigating the toxicity of 6PPD-quinone, a chemical produced by tires, which is highly lethal to fish, including coho salmon, even in small amounts. Studies show the chemical leaches from tires and artificial fields, and researchers are working to find safer alternatives.

Health.headline: New biological marker of early-stage Alzheimer's disease uncovered
Health.detail: A newly discovered biological marker may help in the early detection of Alzheimer's disease, potentially leading to earlier interventions and improved outcomes. The research highlights a specific protein that shows promise in identifying the disease at its initial stages.

Health.headline: Coffee's sweet spot may help mental health in the long run
Health.detail: Recent studies suggest that moderate coffee consumption may have long-term benefits for mental health. The findings indicate a specific range of coffee intake that could positively influence mental well-being.

Health.headline: For first time in months Florida's measles cases stay flat
Health.detail: After months of increases, the number of measles cases in Florida has remained steady, with no new cases reported in Collier County since March 28. As of April 4, there have been 144 cases of reported measles in Florida, making it the state with the fourth most cases in the country for 2026.

Health.headline: UM medical students use virtual reality to explore art, enhancing clinical skills
Health.detail: The University of Miami is using virtual reality to help medical students improve their clinical skills through visual arts education. The "VITaLs" study aims to enhance observation, communication, and perspective-taking abilities, addressing the issue of preventable deaths in hospitals linked to communication failures.

Health.headline: Health care employs 16% of Nanaimo labour force
Health.detail: Health care is a major economic driver in Nanaimo, British Columbia, employing 16% of the labor force, with growth expected due to the construction of a cancer center and plans for a cardiac catheterization lab. Vancouver Island University's nursing program enables local residents to train for well-paying jobs in the healthcare sector without leaving the community.

Sports.headline: Rory McIlroy stumbles at the Masters and now shares the lead with Cameron Young
Sports.detail: Rory McIlroy started the third round of the Masters with a record six-shot lead through 36 holes, but his resilience will be tested again after the lead evaporated.

Sports.headline: Ulberg delivers first-round KO to win light heavyweight belt as Trump takes spotlight at UFC 327
Sports.detail: UFC 327 saw Ulberg win the light heavyweight belt with a first-round knockout, while Donald Trump's presence also garnered attention.

Sports.headline: Shane Lowry makes Masters history: His second hole-in-one at Augusta National earns him crystal bowl
Sports.detail: Shane Lowry made Masters history with his second hole-in-one at Augusta National, earning him a crystal bowl.

Sports.headline: Max Muncy Hits 3 Home Runs, Including Walk Off, to Help Dodgers Top Rangers
Sports.detail: Max Muncy had a stellar performance, hitting 3 home runs, including a walk-off, to lead the Dodgers to victory over the Rangers.

Sports.headline: New Zealand cyclist Kiaan Watts suspended after punching rival rider mid-race
Sports.detail: Kiaan Watts, a cyclist from New Zealand, has been suspended after he punched another rider during a race.

Entertainment.headline: Justin Bieber Speedruns Through His Early Hits Dueting with himself via YouTube at Coachella
Entertainment.detail: Justin Bieber made a comeback at Coachella 2026, dueting with himself via YouTube, revisiting his early hits.

Entertainment.headline: 'I am film industry's last Mughal': The defiant final years of Asha Bhosle and her 80-year journey as an artiste
Entertainment.detail: A look into the life and career of Asha Bhosle, highlighting her 80-year journey as an artist in the film industry.

Entertainment.headline: Coachella 2026: Justin Bieber launches a major comeback in the desert.
Entertainment.detail: Justin Bieber's comeback performance at Coachella 2026 is making headlines.

Entertainment.headline: WH Officials Disagreed W/ Melania's Epstein Statement.
Entertainment.detail: White House officials reportedly disagreed with Melania Trump's statement regarding Jeffrey Epstein.

Entertainment.headline: Pro-Palestinian organizers end Giller Prize boycott, citing successful campaign
Entertainment.detail: Pro-Palestinian organizers have ended their boycott of the Giller Prize, citing a successful campaign.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man arrested for alleged damage to plane at Shannon airport
Crime & Justice.detail: A man in his 40s was arrested for alleged criminal damage to a US military aircraft at Shannon Airport, causing a brief suspension of operations. The man was seen climbing on the plane and hitting it with an object.

Crime & Justice.headline: Prior Notice By Police Not Necessary For Property Attachment U/S 106 BNSS: Allahabad High Court
Crime & Justice.detail: The Allahabad High Court ruled that police are not required to serve prior notice when attaching property under Section 106 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), distinguishing it from Section 107 where a magistrate must issue a notice.

Crime & Justice.headline: London police arrest more than 200 at protest backing banned group Palestine Action
Crime & Justice.detail: London police have arrested more than 200 people during a protest against the ban on the group Palestine Action.

Crime & Justice.headline: Machete-wielding man killed by police in New York's Grand Central station
Crime & Justice.detail: A machete-wielding man was killed by police in New York's Grand Central station after he attacked and wounded three elderly people. Authorities are investigating the incident.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cowboys defensive back Markquese Bell is arrested on drug possession charges near Dallas
Crime & Justice.detail: Cowboys defensive back Markquese Bell was taken into custody near Dallas on drug possession charges.

Education.headline: Education Secretary Used Fake Photo in Post About Black History Icon
Education.detail: Linda McMahon's Truth Social post about journalist Ida B. Wells is the latest problematic use of AI and other digitally created images by the Trump administration.

Education.headline: First grants announced under Labor Dept elementary and secondary ed partnership
Education.detail: The two grant competitions cover literacy in high-need areas and incentives for performance-based compensation for teachers and school leaders.

Education.headline: Wisconsin regents defend firing president
Education.detail: The University of Wisconsin regents cite disputes over AI and other topics in president's firing.

Education.headline: Victories for American Taxpayers: Exposing COVID-Era Fraud, Waste, and Abuse in Federal Education Programs
Education.detail: The Trump Administration continues to tackle fraud, waste, and abuse across federal elementary and secondary education and higher education programs that have squandered taxpayer dollars as part of the President's Task Force to Eliminate Fraud.

Education.headline: What Teachers Should Know About Tax Deductions
Education.detail: Clear criteria and privacy protections are critical when using technology to smooth the hiring process.

Lifestyle.headline: Mother's hand-stitched dress from AI generated image leaves internet stunned
Lifestyle.detail: A creator showed how her mother made a dress, making even the digital distortions faithfully, leaving the internet stunned.

Lifestyle.headline: 75-year-old dadi refuses to slow down at the gym: Inside her impressive workout
Lifestyle.detail: Amrit Kaur, who, at the age of 75 years, started her strength training is inspiring many.

Lifestyle.headline: I Tried and Tested the Shankara Body Wash for two weeks: Here's my honest review
Lifestyle.detail: A review of the Shankara body wash after testing it for two weeks.

Lifestyle.headline: How to capture that perfect photo of Pittsburgh's iconic skyline
Lifestyle.detail: Tips and tricks for capturing the best photo of Pittsburgh's skyline.

Lifestyle.headline: These simple lifestyle changes could decrease dementia risk by 25%
Lifestyle.detail: New research shows that people don't need elaborate or expensive longevity hacks to stay mentally sharp as they age; simple lifestyle changes could decrease dementia risk by 25%.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Subsidies untenable in the long run
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Subsidies for transport can prevent social unrest in the short term, but they drain the national budget and slow down modernization of the public transport system, especially during global crises. The government lacks the resources to sustain fuel subsidies, and this money could be better spent on infrastructure improvements.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: This week's cartoons & Sunday Opinion round-up
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This is a roundup of editorials and commentary from op-ed columnists, the editorial board, and contributing writers featured in the Dallas Morning News. Topics range from why Americans aren't dreaming big anymore to gas prices and electric vehicles.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: A century of flying
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An overview of American Airlines' history discusses its innovations and growth since its establishment, including the introduction of dynamic pricing and yield management after government deregulation in 1978. Today, the airline generates $6 billion annually from its AAdvantage program.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: I just got back from Europe. Anti-American sentiment is on the rise
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Anti-American sentiment is increasing due to actions like threatening a whole civilization, invading Middle Eastern countries based on false information, and involvement in pointless conflicts. A rise in anti-American sentiment is observed in Europe, with polls indicating that half of Europeans view President Trump as an enemy rather than an ally.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Cheng ratified Xi's view of Taiwan
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Chinese Nationalist Party (KMT) Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun framed her visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping as historic, with Xi emphasizing the history of relations. The editorial discusses the implications of this meeting.

World.headline: Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand's North Island
World.detail: Cyclone Vaianu crossed the coast near Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells, causing floods and power outages and leading to hundreds of evacuations.

World.headline: At least 30 killed in crush at historic fortress in Haiti
World.detail: A crush at the Laferriere Citadel in Haiti has resulted in at least 30 deaths.

World.headline: US and Iran fail to reach a deal after marathon talks in Pakistan
World.detail: The US and Iran were unable to reach an agreement after extensive discussions in Pakistan, with each side attributing the failure to the other's inflexibility.

World.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching Easter ceasefire
World.detail: Both Ukraine and Russia are trading accusations of violating the Easter ceasefire, with the Kremlin stating that Russia will not extend the truce unless Kyiv agrees to their terms.

World.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
World.detail: Prince Harry is facing a defamation lawsuit from a charity that he helped establish.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa sacks rights commission chair after constitutional amendment rebuke
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa removed Jessie Majome, the Zimbabwe Human Rights Commission Chairperson, after she criticized the proposed constitutional amendment; lawyers are calling the reassignment unconstitutional and void.

Zimbabwe.headline: Robbers disarm police, steal rifle and patrol vehicle in Harare attack
Zimbabwe.detail: In Harare, robbers disarmed police officers and stole their rifle and patrol vehicle, leading to the hospitalization of the injured officers following the midnight attack.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa fires CIO boss Mangwanya, names diplomat Chikawa as new spy chief
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa dismissed Central Intelligence Organisation (CIO) director-general Fulton Mangwanya and appointed diplomat Chikawa as the new spy chief, without providing an official explanation for the decision.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt Board extends Bulawayo Town Clerk's term to 2030
Zimbabwe.detail: The Local Government Board has extended Christopher Dube's contract as Bulawayo Town Clerk to 2030, resolving months of uncertainty and internal divisions within the city.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim, Zambia seal historic US$2bn railway line deal
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe and Zambia have finalized a historic US$2 billion railway line deal.
